source_status: recovered after prior Sage thread compact/recompact failure

device_provenance.capture_caveat: P02-P07 used monitor logging because Experiment Capture was blocked by a host/firmware command mismatch: host expected !cad,<ms>,temp while flashed firmware appeared to accept !cad,<ms>.

global_caveats: Raw ADC counts and provisional bands are evidence; uncalibrated percent moisture is not used.
P02-P07 are bounded monitor-log segments, not isolated Experiment Capture artifacts.
Lower raw counts mean wetter local probe conditions under the current provisional bands.
Rescue-state plants are useful field behavior evidence but not calibration authorities without controlled follow-up.
Runoff and retained water estimates are kitchen/bench estimates unless explicitly measured.

events.event: Final pre-pull monitor state before removing P01 probes.
Water volume correction: prior P01 rewatering was about 1 US cup, applied slowly and distributed, with no visible runoff.

water_balance.notes: No visible runoff; paper towel under inner pot stayed dry.

findings: Four probe locations did not converge overnight.
s2 remained the higher/drier channel.
s4 remained the lowest/wettest channel, but the overwatered band should be treated as provisional because there was no runoff.
plant_segments.evidence_quality: Useful plant microzone evidence; not uniform calibration.

events.event: Watering lower bound after probes were reported inserted and settled.
Runoff observed.
Post-run estimate: about 0.5 cup runoff, about 1 cup retained.

water_balance.notes: P02 had at least 3x P01 soil volume, but retained roughly the same water volume as P01.

findings: Strong hydrophobic dry soil / preferential flow evidence.
Applied water volume was not retained water volume.
s4 did not provide clean wet-response evidence in this segment.
plant_segments.evidence_quality: Good runoff/water-balance evidence; weak four-probe calibration.

plant_segments.plant: other large pothos, slightly smaller than P02

events.event: Probes inserted.
Watering started.
First runoff / initial stop.
Targeted S4 diagnostic pour; later S3/S4 readings treated as contact-confounded.
Segment end / probes pulled.
Runoff measured at about 1/3 cup.

water_balance.notes: Not captured does not prove uniformly absorbed.

findings: P03 repeated P02's parched-pothos runoff/channeling pattern.
S1/S2/S3 initial wet response was coherent.
S3/S4 after the targeted pour are not clean moisture truth.
plant_segments.evidence_quality: Good runoff evidence; S3/S4 later segment contaminated by handling/contact effects.

events.event: Probes inserted / settling started.
Watering/runoff observed after up to 1 cup.
Segment end / probes pulled.

water_balance.notes: Some runoff later appeared to wick back from the temporary bowl.

findings: Clear insert showed water reaching the bowl while soil did not visibly soak evenly.
Shallow pot and partial probe insertion reduce calibration confidence.
Board/breadboard had direct skylight context during the between-plant/P04 window, then was moved out of the direct shaft.
plant_segments.evidence_quality: Strong physical bypass evidence; low calibration confidence.

events.event: Only two probes inserted due to very tight rootball.
Watering start.
First runoff / bypass after about 1/2 cup.
Probe pull / transition to P06; user confirmed inserted probes were s1 and s4.
Post-pull observation: roots extended outside pot into bowl water.

water_balance.notes: About half of the estimated bypass may have been reabsorbed through escaped roots in the bowl.

findings: P05 surface beaded water and sent it down the sides.
Inserted probes s1 and s4 still looked dry/air-dry because the hard rootball/contact geometry dominated the reading.
Escaped roots in the bowl may be a primary water uptake path.
plant_segments.evidence_quality: High plant-condition evidence; not clean soil calibration.

events.event: P06 setup marker.
Watering start after radial probe insertion.
First dose of about 1.0 to 1.25 cups; minor seepage.
Second-dose start after early seepage reabsorbed.
Sustained pooling at about 1 2/3 cups total.
S1 wetward response visible.
S2 wetward response visible.
Probe pull / segment end.

water_balance.notes: Early seepage was reabsorbed within roughly 1 to 2 minutes; final sustained pooling remained.

findings: P6 accepted staged watering better than the bypass-heavy P02/P04/P05 plants.
Response was still uneven; s1 responded first and most clearly.
Pulse-and-pause watering is supported for this plant/pot condition.

plant_segments.segment_type: measure-only baseline after hidden standing-water cachepot finding

events.event: P07 prep/photo context; visually bromeliad/rosette-like.
Standing water found in outer/cachepot despite about 3 weeks without watering.
Probes inserted; no watering.
Measure-only baseline resumed after app restart.
Probes pulled with no water added; sensors to be wiped and left to return to open-air dry state before the next plant.
Post-pull inspection found P07 s3 was barely inserted with no real depth or soil contact; exclude s3 from P07 soil interpretation.

water_balance.notes: No watering added. Hidden standing water in the cachepot from an older watering overrode the generic dry-soil watering impulse.

invalid_channels.s3: Post-pull inspection found s3 was barely inserted, shallow, and not meaningfully coupled to soil.

findings: Local probe zones read dry to needs-water, but hidden standing water means lower roots may have been wet or oxygen-limited.
No water was added; the standing-water cachepot observation overrode the dry local probe readings.
P07 s3 is contact-invalid and should be excluded from soil interpretation.
P7 should not be treated like the parched pothos/dracaena rescue cases without checking cachepot design and lower-root condition.
plant_segments.evidence_quality: Important cachepot/standing-water evidence; no-water decision recorded; s3 contact-invalid.

inventory_plan_from_thread.P08: very small cactus, not yet captured in recovered evidence; described before measurement as green triangular base with pink round top and three smaller pink side domes, likely grafted cactus form but ID not confirmed
inventory_plan_from_thread.P09: very small succulent, not yet captured in recovered evidence
inventory_plan_from_thread.P10: former work plant, better maintained, about 1 cup/week for about 4 years
inventory_plan_from_thread.P11: former work plant, better maintained, about 1 cup/week for about 4 years

survey_level_implications: Applied water volume is not retained water volume.
Runoff does not prove a pot is uniformly watered.
Probe readings are local and can be contact-confounded.
Pump logic should prefer small pulses with settle/soak pauses.
Rootbound/cachepot conditions require plant-specific safety logic.
open_followups: For P07, keep the cachepot standing-water issue as primary: dry outside the cachepot, add a riser/pebbles or other air gap, and only consider a later measured micro-dose after lower-root condition is checked.
Continue with P08 as a one-probe tiny-cactus measurement; leave unused sensors open-air dry as references.
Use P10/P11 as better-maintained contrast plants in later controlled runs.
Route the Experiment Capture command mismatch to Firmware/Data.
Do not use P02-P07 rescue survey as A2/C1 calibration authority without controlled follow-up.
